summary: Jeff Atwood shares a small discovery he made while examining a Windows ME screenshot in a Joel Spolsky article. He noticed Qantas icons that were shortcuts to a Unix machine at Fog Creek Software. After investigating, he realized the machine name 'Qantas' was a clever reference to the movie Rain Man, where the character Raymond Babbitt obsessively insists that Qantas has never crashed. Atwood concludes with appreciation for Spolsky's sense of humor, noting he must be fun to work with.
key_points: A Unix machine at Fog Creek Software was named 'Qantas' based on evidence in old screenshots and forum posts
The name is a pun referencing Rain Man, where the autistic savant character famously declares 'QANTAS never crashed'
Small details in screenshots and documentation can reveal personality and culture of a workplace
Atwood expresses respect for Spolsky's humor even when disagreeing with his technical opinions
money_quotes: Even if I don't always agree with him, the guy has got to be fun to work with.
key_insight: The small, nerdy cultural references hidden in a company's technical infrastructure reveal something endearing about the people who work there.
